data.narrative:
Reverion, a Series B climate and energy company, carries a current post-money valuation of $310M on $79M total raised. The valuation universe comprises five same-stage, same-sector private peers (Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B, Pano AI at $500M, Sage Geosystems at $400M, CarbonCapture Inc. at $300M, and Cemvita Factory at $120M), plus one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $310M base, yielding $589M. The peer-median approach across the five-company climate and energy cohort produces $400M, reflecting the median of a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. This dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within the sector.

Synthesizing these approaches yields a valuation range of $340M (low) to $677M (high), with a median of $494M. The current $310M valuation sits meaningfully below both implied estimates, suggesting undervaluation relative to stage and peer benchmarks. Key uncertainties include revenue traction and path to commercialization relative to the peer set, particularly given the wide range within the climate-tech cohort, and the degree to which Commonwealth Fusion Systems' $5.0B valuation reflects sector-wide enthusiasm versus company-specific factors.
